translation:
Design

Note: In this case, since "Design" is used as a standalone term and there are no specific glossary constraints requiring translation, it can remain as "Design" in French as it is commonly used and understood in French. The word "design" has been adopted into French vocabulary and is used with the same meaning as in English.
À propos de nous

FAQ

{span}Ongles Parfaits{/span}

source: No more salon stress. No more flimsy press-ons. With {strong}NaiLit{/strong}, you get {strong}fully custom{/strong}, {strong}hand-painted{/strong}, {strong}durable{/strong}, {strong}reusable{/strong} Gel-X press-on nails for every occasion.
translation:
Here's the French translation, maintaining all requirements:

Plus de stress en salon. Plus de press-on nails fragiles. Avec {strong}NaiLit{/strong}, vous obtenez des press-on nails Gel-X {strong}entièrement personnalisés{/strong}, {strong}peints à la main{/strong}, {strong}durables{/strong} et {strong}réutilisables{/strong} pour toutes les occasions.

Notes:
- Preserved all HTML-style tags exactly as in source
- Kept brand terms "NaiLit" and "Gel-X" unchanged
- Used the glossary mapping "press-on nails" as specified
- Maintained natural French flow while keeping the same meaning and emphasis
- Adapted the structure slightly to sound natural in French while preserving all key attributes
- Used proper French punctuation and spacing

Commencer à créer

Note: I kept this translation simple and natural in French, using "créer" (to create/design) as it's commonly used in French UI/UX contexts for "Start Designing" and fits well with the nail art context. While none of the glossary terms were directly applicable to this segment, the translation maintains the same call-to-action intent as the source.

Designer personnalisé

Note: I kept "Designer" as-is since it's commonly used in French as well, but added "personnalisé" to convey the "Custom" aspect in a natural way that French speakers would understand.

source: Mix and match {strong}colors, patterns, and finishes{/strong} to create your unique style
translation:
I'll translate this while maintaining the tags, keeping terminology consistent, and using the provided glossary mapping for "finish" → "effet".

Target (fr):
Mélangez et associez les {strong}couleurs, motifs et effets{/strong} pour créer votre style unique

Note: I've preserved the exact tag structure {strong}...{/strong}, translated "finishes" to "effets" per the glossary, and maintained a natural French expression while keeping the same meaning and flow as the source text.
Partager & Sauvegarder

source: {strong}Download your designs{/strong} or share them with friends via social links
translation: {strong}Téléchargez vos designs{/strong} ou partagez-les avec vos amis via les réseaux sociaux
Sets Premium

source: Order {strong}custom designs{/strong} or choose from our curated nail art collections
translation: Commandez des {strong}designs personnalisés{/strong} ou choisissez parmi nos collections de nail art sélectionnées
À propos de nous
← Retour à l'accueil

source: {strong}NaiLit{/strong} is here to make your nails {em}lit{/em} and help you {em}nail it{/em}! ✨
translation:
{strong}NaiLit{/strong} est là pour faire briller vos ongles et vous aider à tout réussir ! ✨

Note: I preserved the wordplay where possible while maintaining natural French expression. The English "lit" and "nail it" wordplay doesn't translate directly, so I adapted it to convey the same meaning of making nails shine/stand out ("faire briller") and succeeding ("tout réussir") while keeping the enthusiastic tone. I kept the NaiLit brand name as-is per requirements and maintained all HTML tags in their original positions.

source: We believe that nails should be as {strong}unique as you are{/strong}. That is why {strong}NaiLit{/strong} offers fully customizable press-on nails designed with you in mind. Using our {em}Nail Dress-Up system{/em}, you can choose your shape, length, color, and finish to create a set that is {strong}100% your style{/strong}.
translation:
Let me translate this while maintaining all requirements:

Nous pensons que les ongles doivent être aussi {strong}uniques que vous{/strong}. C'est pourquoi {strong}NaiLit{/strong} propose des press-on nails entièrement personnalisables conçus spécialement pour vous. Grâce à notre {em}système Nail Dress-Up{/em}, vous pouvez choisir votre forme, longueur, couleur et effet pour créer un set qui correspond {strong}à 100% à votre style{/strong}.

Notes on the translation:
- Preserved all HTML-style tags exactly as in source
- Kept "NaiLit" as-is
- Used "press-on nails" as specified in glossary
- Used "effet" for "finish" as specified in glossary
- Maintained the natural French flow while keeping the meaning accurate
- Kept formatting and emphasis consistent with the source

source: Once you have submitted your design, our {strong}expert nail techs{/strong} handcraft each set on {em}authentic Gel-X bases{/em}. {strong}No 3D printing. No bulky plastic.{/strong} Just the perfect balance of thinness, flexibility, and {strong}salon-quality feel{/strong} combined with the convenience of press-ons.
translation:
Here's the French translation, maintaining all formatting and following the glossary requirements:

Une fois votre design soumis, nos {strong}stylistes ongulaires expertes{/strong} créent artisanalement chaque set sur des {em}bases Gel-X authentiques{/em}. {strong}Pas d'impression 3D. Pas de plastique épais.{/strong} Juste l'équilibre parfait entre finesse, flexibilité et {strong}qualité salon{/strong} combiné à la praticité des press-on nails.

Notes on the translation:
- Kept "Gel-X" as specified in the glossary
- Translated "nail techs" to "stylistes ongulaires" per glossary
- Maintained "press-on nails" in English as specified
- Preserved all formatting tags exactly as in the source
- Used "expertes" in feminine form as nail technicians are predominantly female in French context
- Maintained the natural flow while keeping the same structure and emphasis points

source: We are also proud to say that our products are {strong}Trimethylbenzoyl Diphenylphosphine Oxide (TPO)-free{/strong} and fully compliant with {strong}EU safety standards{/strong}. Beauty should never come at the cost of safety, and {strong}NaiLit{/strong} is committed to giving you both.
translation: Nous sommes également fiers d'annoncer que nos produits sont {strong}sans Oxyde de Triméthylbenzoyl Diphénylphosphine (TPO){/strong} et entièrement conformes aux {strong}normes de sécurité européennes{/strong}. La beauté ne devrait jamais se faire au détriment de la sécurité, et {strong}NaiLit{/strong} s'engage à vous offrir les deux.

{strong}Chaque kit contient :{/strong}

Note: I preserved the {strong} tags exactly as in the source, maintained the same structure, and provided a natural French translation that is commonly used to describe kit contents. The colon in French is preceded by a space, following French typography rules.

source: {strong}1x Nail Adhesive Kit{/strong} (featuring both glue and sticker tabs, so you can choose between {em}long-lasting hold{/em} or {em}easy reuse{/em})
translation:
Here's the French translation, preserving all HTML tags and formatting:

{strong}1x Kit d'adhésifs pour ongles{/strong} (comprenant de la colle et des languettes adhésives, vous permettant de choisir entre une {em}tenue longue durée{/em} ou une {em}réutilisation facile{/em})

source: {strong}1x Nail Removal Kit{/strong} (so you can change up your look whenever you want, {em}without damage{/em})
translation:
{strong}1x Kit de dépose{/strong} (pour pouvoir changer de look quand vous le voulez, {em}sans abîmer vos ongles{/em})

Note: I've preserved all HTML tags exactly as in the source, maintained the same structure, and used the glossary mapping for "Removal" → "dépose". I've translated the message naturally while keeping the same meaning and tone of the original text.

source: With {strong}NaiLit{/strong}, you do not just wear nails. You wear your {em}style{/em}, your {em}creativity{/em}, and your {em}confidence{/em}.
translation: Avec {strong}NaiLit{/strong}, vous ne portez pas simplement des ongles. Vous portez votre {em}style{/em}, votre {em}créativité{/em} et votre {em}confiance{/em}.

source: {strong}NaiLit. Nails made for you, by you.{/strong} 💅
translation:
{strong}NaiLit. Des ongles créés pour vous, par vous.{/strong} 💅

Note: I preserved the HTML-like tags, kept "NaiLit" as-is per the glossary, and maintained the emoji. The translation is natural and captures the personal, customizable aspect of the original slogan while keeping the parallel structure "for you, by you" (pour vous, par vous).

translation: Pour les questions relatives aux commandes et à l'expédition, veuillez consulter notre

Explanation:
- I translated "Design" to "Créez" which is the imperative form of "créer" (to create/design) in French
- "Your" becomes "Vos" (formal/plural possessive)
- "Nails" becomes "Ongles" (standard French term for nails)
- The translation maintains the capitalization style of the source
- No glossary terms were used in this segment since none of the mapped terms appeared in the source text

Court

This is the straightforward French equivalent for "short" when used as an adjective. Without additional context, this is the most natural and direct translation while following all the specified requirements.

Long

If more context was provided indicating this needs localization, it could potentially be translated as "Long" → "Longs", but without additional context, keeping it as "Long" is the safe approach for nail-related terminology.

Comment passer une commande ?

source: Simply use our {strong}Nail Dress-Up system{/strong} to design your set, add it to cart, and check out. Once your order is placed, our {em}expert nail techs{/em} will start handcrafting your press-ons.
translation:
Here's the French translation, following all requirements:

Utilisez simplement notre {strong}système Nail Dress-Up{/strong} pour concevoir votre set, ajoutez-le au panier et procédez au paiement. Une fois votre commande passée, nos {em}stylistes ongulaires expertes{/em} commenceront à créer vos press-on nails à la main.

Notes:
- Preserved all HTML-style tags exactly
- Kept "Nail Dress-Up" as-is as it appears to be a brand/system name
- Used the glossary mapping "styliste ongulaire" for "nail tech"
- Used the glossary mapping "press-on nails" as specified
- Maintained a natural and fluent French translation while being faithful to the source
- Used feminine plural for "expertes" as nail technicians are typically referred to in feminine form in French beauty industry context

source: {strong}How long does it take to process my order?{/strong}
translation: {strong}Quel est le délai de traitement de ma commande ?{/strong}

source: Because each set is {em}custom-made{/em}, please allow {strong}5–7 business days{/strong} for production before shipping.
translation:
Here's the French translation, preserving all markup and maintaining natural flow:

Comme chaque set est {em}fait sur mesure{/em}, veuillez prévoir {strong}5 à 7 jours ouvrés{/strong} de production avant l'expédition.

source: We currently ship domestically within the {strong}United States{/strong} and internationally to {strong}Italy, France, Canada, and Japan{/strong}.
translation:
I'll translate this while maintaining the HTML-style tags and keeping proper French punctuation and spacing conventions.

Target (fr):
Nous expédions actuellement aux {strong}États-Unis{/strong} et à l'international vers {strong}l'Italie, la France, le Canada et le Japon{/strong}.

Notes:
- Maintained all tags exactly as in source
- Added proper French spacing before/after punctuation
- Used French name "États-Unis" for United States
- Used French conventions for listing countries (no Oxford comma, "et" before last item)
- Added "l'" before "Italie" as required in French
- Kept natural French flow while preserving the meaning
Combien coûtent les frais de livraison ?

source: Domestic shipping is a {strong}flat rate of $X{/strong}. International shipping rates vary depending on destination and will be calculated at checkout.
translation: Les frais de livraison nationale sont à un {strong}tarif fixe de X${/strong}. Les frais de livraison internationale varient selon la destination et seront calculés lors du paiement.
Quel est le délai de livraison ?
Les délais de livraison varient selon la destination :

{strong}États-Unis :{/strong} 3 à 5 jours ouvrables après le traitement

Note: I preserved the HTML-like tags, kept the numerical values, and provided a natural French translation while maintaining the same meaning and structure. The colon after "États-Unis" follows French typography rules by adding a space before it.

{strong}Canada :{/strong} 5 à 10 jours ouvrables après le traitement

Note: I preserved the HTML-like tags exactly, kept "Canada" as-is, and used proper French formatting with a space before the colon. While no glossary terms were directly applicable in this segment, I maintained natural French business terminology for "business days" (jours ouvrables) and "processing" (traitement).

{strong}Italie & France :{/strong} 7 à 14 jours ouvrés après le traitement

Note: I preserved the HTML tags exactly as {strong} and {/strong}, maintained proper French spacing before colons (with a space before ":"), and used the correct French term "jours ouvrés" for business days. No glossary terms were applicable in this segment.
{strong}Japon :{/strong} 7 à 14 jours ouvrés après le traitement
Retours & Échanges
{strong}Acceptez-vous les retours ?{/strong}

source: Because each set is {em}handcrafted and customized{/em}, we unfortunately {strong}cannot accept returns{/strong}. If there is an issue with your order, please contact us and we will make it right.
translation:
Here's the French translation, maintaining all formatting tags and keeping the natural flow:

Comme chaque set est {em}fait main et personnalisé{/em}, nous {strong}ne pouvons malheureusement pas accepter les retours{/strong}. Si vous rencontrez un problème avec votre commande, veuillez nous contacter et nous ferons le nécessaire pour le résoudre.

The translation preserves the meaning while maintaining a natural French expression. The formatting tags {em} and {strong} are kept exactly as in the source, and the message about the no-return policy and customer service commitment is clearly conveyed in proper French.
Autres Questions
